first_blockers: G=raw checkpoint/sample provenance absent; H=raw target checkpoint identity and raw sample IDs are absent; I=Missing raw target checkpoint identity and raw sample IDs; the packet binds tensor roles and metrics, not raw target/sample provenance.
notes: G: Public supplement identity and tensor-role binding are adequate for feature-packet-level review, and replay metrics are hash-aligned. However raw target checkpoint identity and raw sample IDs are absent, and the materials explicitly keep this outside the N50 denominator / external adjudication boundary; use only as bounded Research-side support. | H: Yes for false-promotion utility: the public packet is independently identifiable and binds train/eval member/external feature tensors to replayed metrics, so it is useful as a bounded-support review row that demonstrates strong metrics with partial target/provenance gates. It must not be promoted to admitted, N50 denominator, black-box response, or raw provenance evidence. | I: Usable as bounded Research-side feature-packet evidence for a false-promotion review row only. CCF-A wording risk is material if it is presented as an external benchmark, N50 denominator row, black-box response benchmark, Platform/Runtime admitted row, or fully raw-asset-reproducible result.
